content.full_text:
July 7, 1992

Mr. John Wallace, Attorney  
Fair Political Practices Commission  
P.O. Box 807  
Sacramento, CA 95804

Dear Mr. Wallace:

The purpose of this letter is to confirm our conversation of July 3, 1992, regarding the Wine Institute paying for travel expenses incurred by me and a member of my staff to the National Conference of State Legislatures (NCSL) Conference in Cincinnati, Ohio. I will be gone from July 25, 1992, through July 30, 1992. Rebecca Robinson, a member of my staff, will be traveling from July 25, 1992, through July 27, 1992.

As Chairman of the NCSL Task Force on the Wine Industry, I will be conducting the National Task Force Meeting on Sunday, July 26, 1992. Rebecca Robinson will be speaking on the status of an on-going project to streamline monthly reporting for California wineries who do business out-of-state. Please note that participation on this task force benefits the entire California wine industry. The Wine Institute is a non-profit organization.

Thank you for your prompt attention to this matter. If I am mistaken on the details of our conversation, please advise me immediately.

Sincerely,

DOMINIC L. CORTESE  
Assemblyman, 24th District
